title: Empty repo summer planner interview
repo_state: Empty Git repo named Summer app with no README, app files, or context folder.
prompt: Use $arch. I want to build a summer app.

turns.repo_inspection: Repo inspection: empty Git repo with no app decisions to preserve.
turns.question: Question 1/7: Which first user workflow should v1 make durable, and who owns it?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: A summer planning web app for one friend-group organizer who needs to collect ideas, availability, and decisions in one place.

options.label: Second option
options.text: A family summer activity planner for one parent coordinating activities, budget, and dates.

options.label: Other
options.text: Use a different product and target user in one sentence.

turns.why_recommended: It narrows v1 to one user and one painful coordination job instead of a broad social platform.
turns.architecture_impact: Locks the MVP workflow owner in context/project-overview.md and drives the first durable source-of-truth decision in context/architecture-context.md.

turns.confirmed_decision: Product: summer planning web app. First target user: friend-group organizer coordinating ideas, availability, and decisions.

context_updates.summary: Record product, target user, MVP boundary, and early scope cut away from social feed features.
Record confirmed decision and keep remaining architecture questions open.

next_question.question: Question 2/7: Which completed action proves the planning workflow and becomes the first feature spec?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: Organizer sends one planning link and gets at least three friends to submit availability and vote on one idea.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Organizer creates a shared trip board with ideas and notes but no voting yet.

options.label: Other
options.text: Use a different value proof in one sentence.

next_question.why_recommended: It proves coordination value with the smallest measurable loop.
next_question.architecture_impact: Locks the measurable success criterion in context/project-overview.md and the first feature spec in context/feature-specs/.

turns.confirmed_decision: Value proof: one shared planning link produces availability and votes from at least three friends.

context_updates.summary: Add success criteria and the core organizer-to-friend loop.
Draft the first buildable feature around creating and sharing a plan.

next_question.question: Question 3/7: Which system boundary should own organizer and invitee interactions in v1?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: Responsive web app with organizer and invitee views.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Single-page static prototype with local mock data.

options.label: Other
options.text: Use a different interface in one sentence.

next_question.why_recommended: A web app is the smallest shareable surface for group coordination.
next_question.architecture_impact: Locks the app/interface boundary in context/architecture-context.md and the route/view split in context/ui-context.md.

red_flags_absent: asks all intake questions at once
starts coding before confirmed product and value proof
recommends native mobile before proving the share loop

title: Existing Next.js SaaS rescue interview
repo_state: Existing Next.js app with Supabase auth, a Stripe dependency, several unfinished dashboard routes, and no context folder.
prompt: Use $arch to inspect this repo and create context so Codex can finish the MVP without drifting.

turns.repo_inspection: Repo inspection: existing Next.js/Supabase app with unfinished dashboard routes and no context folder.
turns.question: Question 1/6: Which existing workflow should Codex complete first, and which stack decisions must it preserve?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: Support manager reviews new escalations, assigns an owner, and marks one escalation resolved.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Admin configures organization settings and team roles.

options.label: Other
options.text: Use a different first workflow in one sentence.

turns.why_recommended: It finishes one operational loop and delays admin surface area until the core value is proven.
turns.architecture_impact: Locks the preservation boundary for the current stack in context/architecture-context.md and the first rescue feature spec.

turns.confirmed_decision: First workflow: support manager reviews, assigns, and resolves an escalation.

context_updates.summary: Record MVP workflow, target user, and scope cuts for admin settings and broad dashboards.
Preserve existing Next.js and Supabase decisions instead of recommending a rewrite.
Record current phase as repo rescue and next decision as data boundaries.

next_question.question: Question 2/6: Which escalation data must be durable, and where should mutations be validated?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: Real Supabase escalation records with status, owner, priority, customer, and audit timestamps.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Mock escalation records in code while finishing UI flow first.

options.label: Other
options.text: Use a different data boundary in one sentence.

next_question.why_recommended: This workflow is only valuable if assignments and status changes persist.
next_question.architecture_impact: Locks the durable data model, mutation validation boundary, and feature spec requirements.

turns.confirmed_decision: Escalations are real Supabase records with status, owner, priority, customer, and audit timestamps.

context_updates.summary: Define escalation table fields, server-side validation, and Supabase access boundary.
Create the buildable implementation unit for list, assign, resolve, and verify.

next_question.question: Question 3/6: What billing boundary should v1 preserve while the operational workflow is unfinished?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: Keep Stripe dependency untouched and manually manage access until the resolution loop works.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Wire a basic Stripe checkout before finishing the operational workflow.

options.label: Other
options.text: Use a different billing decision in one sentence.

next_question.why_recommended: Billing is premature until the core support workflow proves value.
next_question.architecture_impact: Locks billing as out of scope or preserved dependency in context/architecture-context.md and context/progress-tracker.md.

red_flags_absent: prioritizes billing before the core workflow
creates context without preserving existing repo decisions

title: AI founder spec generator interview
repo_state: New product repo with only the prompt idea and no implementation files.
prompt: Use $arch. Build a product where founders turn messy startup ideas into specs AI coding agents can implement.

turns.repo_inspection: Repo inspection: no existing product or stack decisions, so v1 can be scoped from first principles.
turns.question: Question 1/7: Which user-owned output must v1 make trustworthy, and what does the system store?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: Solo founder with a messy idea who needs one implementation-ready feature spec for Codex.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Startup team that needs a full PRD, roadmap, and investor-facing strategy doc.

options.label: Other
options.text: Use a different first user and trusted output in one sentence.

turns.why_recommended: One founder and one buildable spec is easier to verify than a broad strategy platform.
turns.architecture_impact: Locks the trusted AI output boundary in context/project-overview.md and the storage/review model in context/architecture-context.md.

turns.confirmed_decision: First user: solo founder. Trusted output: one implementation-ready feature spec for Codex.

context_updates.summary: Record user, outcome, MVP boundary, and scope cut away from roadmap and investor docs.
Record that generated specs must be small enough for a coding assistant to execute.

next_question.question: Question 2/7: Which AI boundary should v1 own before generated content becomes trusted state?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: Ask clarifying questions, extract decisions, and draft one feature spec with assumptions and verification steps.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Generate a full product plan, architecture, backlog, and launch copy in one pass.

options.label: Other
options.text: Use a different AI boundary in one sentence.

next_question.why_recommended: A constrained AI workflow is easier to review and safer than one-shot product generation.
next_question.architecture_impact: Locks model responsibilities, validation, review state, and trusted-output rules in context/architecture-context.md.

turns.confirmed_decision: AI boundary: clarify, extract decisions, and draft one feature spec with assumptions and verification steps.

context_updates.summary: Define AI as a server-side drafting workflow with persisted inputs, outputs, and review state.
Create the first buildable unit from idea intake to reviewed feature spec.

next_question.question: Question 3/7: What data and privacy boundary protects founder ideas and generated specs?

options.label: Recommended
options.text: Store idea text, extracted decisions, generated spec, and user-owned project records; do not train on user content.

options.label: Second option
options.text: Keep everything local in browser storage for a prototype.

options.label: Other
options.text: Use a different data and privacy boundary in one sentence.

next_question.why_recommended: Founders need persistence and trust, while the no-training rule keeps the security boundary clear.
next_question.architecture_impact: Locks sensitive data handling, retention, privacy rules, and AI-output persistence.

red_flags_absent: feature spec too large for a coding assistant
